page: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is a High Performance family virtual server featuring 12 shared vCPUs mapped from 6 physical Intel Xeon cores with hyper-threading enabled. It operates on the x86_64 architecture at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, supported by 16 MB of L3 cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. The system is configured with 24.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, and includes 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark evaluations place this server in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. In stress-ng testing, it achieves a single-core score of 1418.58 and a multi-core score of 17309.27. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1224.9 GB/sec, dropping to 87.74 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, with large block write speeds at 47.62 GB/sec. The server demonstrates solid database and web serving capabilities, processing 18.53 million Redis SET operations per second and maintaining a static web serving throughput of 11.99 GB/sec.

While the server handles lightweight machine learning tasks adequately, processing 719.57 prompt tokens per second on a small 135M LLM model, it exhibits performance limitations on larger models, dropping to a weak 7.43 tokens per second for text generation on a 7B model. The shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for sustained, high-intensity compute workloads. Given its 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage, this configuration is suitable for web serving, database operations, and general-purpose multi-threaded applications that do not require dedicated CPU cores.
description: The Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is an x86_64 virtual server from the High Performance family, equipped with 12 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. It features 24.0 GB of RAM, 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 16 MB L3 cache. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard operations, with a PassMark CPU score of 17934.88 and a Redis SET throughput of 18.53 million operations per second. While memory bandwidth is strong for cached reads at 1224.9 GB/sec, large-block uncached writes operate at 47.62 GB/sec. The server is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and lightweight text processing, though its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio make it less optimal for memory-intensive tasks.
og_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is an x86_64 High Performance server featuring 12 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 24.0 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is an x86_64 server with 12 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe storage, optimized for general compute workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized virtual server featuring twelve shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, local NVMe storage, and balanced multi-threaded performance.
bullet_points: 12 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled
24.0 GB of system memory and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 18.53 million Redis SET operations per second
Achieves 11.99 GB/sec static web serving throughput
Suited for web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded utility tasks
